commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof and normal maintainer review of the v2 compatibility tradeoff, not an automated repair task.; Cleared: The diff touches auth signing but reuses the existing v2 payload contract and adds no new dependency, CI, secret, or artifact execution path; no concrete security or supply-chain regression was found. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Swift v2 device-auth payload builder, switches Swift gateway connect signing from v3 to v2, adds reset helpers, and adds a Swift payload-vector test.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current Swift signs only buildV3 while the gateway still has a v2 canonical payload contract and dual verifier. I did not establish a live failing old-gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof and normal maintainer review of the v2 compatibility tradeoff, not an automated repair task.
